steps.title: Spot the data that determines number
steps.content: The passage explicitly states two different word counts for the two constitutions: Costa Rica's at 16,705 words and Portugal's at 35,219 words. This establishes that we're discussing two distinct, separate lengths—not a single combined length measure.

steps.title: Apply the plural possessive rule
steps.content: The sentence needs “these [blank]” as the subject of “correlate.” Since the lengths belong to and vary among the two constitutions, we need plural possessive “constitutions'” paired with plural noun “lengths.” Choices A and B incorrectly use singular “length” despite having two distinct word counts; choice D uses plural “lengths” but omits the possessive form needed to clearly show the lengths belong to the constitutions. That matches choice C — C is correct.
